perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video opens with a graphic showing a hand holding a dark century egg against a solid red background. (2) A young woman is then shown sitting on a sofa, biting into a real century egg with a thoughtful expression. (3) The scene switches to a yellow background where a hand holds a piece of pineapple cake. (4) The woman bites the pineapple cake, looking confused as a text bubble appears asking why it sounds like chips. (5) She is shown with a puzzled look, surrounded by question marks and onomatopoeia graphics. (6) She is then seen behind a table overflowing with bags of 'Shui Shui Kitchen' potato chips, holding a bag in each hand. (7) A split-screen displays her eating chips from the 'Century Egg' flavor bag on the left and the 'Pineapple Cake' flavor bag on the right. (8) The final shot shows her giving a thumbs up while holding the product bags with promotional text overlay.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, high-energy electronic pop music starts immediately and continues throughout the video. (2) Distinct, loud 'crunch' sound effects are played whenever the woman bites into the food items, creating a sensory contrast with the soft foods initially shown. (3) An enthusiastic, fast-paced female voice-over in Mandarin enters, introducing the new potato chip flavors and their availability. (4) The music and voice-over maintain a consistent high volume until the video ends.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) '皮蛋' (Century Egg) and '鳳梨酥' (Pineapple Cake) appear as identifying labels. (2) '為什麼會有洋芋片的聲音?!' (Why is there the sound of potato chips?!) appears as a hook to generate curiosity. (3) '酥', '喀', '脆', '滋' (Crunchy, crackling sound effects) are decorative text used to emphasize the texture. (4) '皮蛋跟鳳梨酥風味洋芋片新上市!' (Century Egg and Pineapple Cake flavor potato chips newly launched!) serves as the main functional announcement. (5) '7/3 全家門市限定販售!' (7/3 FamilyMart stores limited sale!) provides functional purchasing information.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: The video follows a Hook (strange sounds from soft food) -> Reveal (new chip flavors) -> Call to Action (store availability) flow.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator is shown throughout the video.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with dark hair tied back, wearing a white spaghetti strap top decorated with yellow flowers.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Alternates between solid color graphic backgrounds (red, yellow) and a bright, modern living room interior with a beige sofa, a full-length mirror, and a table.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Bright, even studio lighting with a warm feel.

audio_production_style.audioTransition: Hard cut transitions between scenes, synced with musical beats.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video aims to promote the launch of 'Shui Shui Kitchen' branded potato chips featuring unique traditional Taiwanese flavors (Century Egg and Pineapple Cake). It uses a sensory hook by associating the crunching sound of chips with the visual of soft foods to pique interest before presenting the actual product and its exclusive availability at FamilyMart.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The creator is actively presenting the product bags, speaking about the launch, and the video concludes with retail location information.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: High-energy Mandarin voiceover with matching high-paced beat-synced editing.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video successfully uses ASMR-style 'crunch' sounds to bridge the gap between the unusual food flavors and the medium of potato chips.
